RRB ALP Recruitment 2026

The Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) under the Ministry of Railways have released the detailed Centralised Employment Notification (CEN No. 01/2026) for 11,127 Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP) posts across Indian Railways. Online applications open on 15 May 2026 and close on 14 June 2026 at 23:59 hours, with the last date for fee payment being 16 June 2026.

The indicative notice for this recruitment was published on 21 March 2026, and the detailed notification has now been released by the Chairpersons of all participating RRBs. Candidates can apply to only one RRB, and the choice of RRB once made cannot be changed at any later stage. A modification window will be open from 17 June 2026 to 26 June 2026 for any corrections in the submitted application, with a non-refundable modification fee of ₹250 per occasion.

Note: Candidates can submit only one application against this CEN. Multiple applications, whether to the same RRB or different RRBs, will lead to rejection of all such applications and debarment from future RRB and RRC examinations.

If you are looking for other Railway Recruitment notifications such as RRB NTPC, RRB Group D, or RRB JE, we have separate dedicated pages for those. Visit the RRB NTPC Recruitment, RRB Group D Recruitment, or RRB JE Recruitment page for more details.

RRB ALP Recruitment Details

Post Name: Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP)

CEN Number: 01/2026

Total Posts: 11,127 Posts

Organization: Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs), Ministry of Railways, Government of India

Pay Level: Level 2 as per 7th CPC Pay Matrix

Initial Pay: ₹19,900 per month plus allowances such as Dearness Allowance, House Rent Allowance, and Transport Allowance as applicable

Age Limit: 18 to 30 years as on 1 July 2026. Age relaxation is applicable for SC/ST (5 years), OBC-NCL (3 years), Ex-Servicemen (3 to 8 years after deducting length of service), and other eligible categories as per Railway rules. You can use our Age Calculator to check if you meet the age requirement.

Educational Qualification: Matriculation / SSLC plus ITI from a recognised institution (NCVT/SCVT) in the trades of Fitter, Electrician, Instrument Mechanic, Millwright/Maintenance Mechanic, Mechanic (Radio & TV), Electronics Mechanic, Mechanic (Motor Vehicle), Wireman, Tractor Mechanic, Armature & Coil Winder, Mechanic (Diesel), Heat Engine, Turner, Machinist, or Refrigeration & Air-Conditioning Mechanic. Candidates with Matriculation plus Course Completed Act Apprenticeship in the above trades are also eligible. Alternatively, candidates having Matriculation plus a three-year Diploma or Degree (B.E./B.Tech) in Mechanical, Electrical, Electronics, or Automobile Engineering from a recognised institution can apply in lieu of ITI.

Medical Standard: A-1 as per the Indian Railway Medical Manual. Candidates must have unaided distant vision of 6/6 in both eyes (without glasses, with fogging test, not accepting +2D), near vision of Sn 0.6 in both eyes without glasses, and must pass tests for colour vision, binocular vision, field of vision, night vision, and mesopic vision. Candidates who have undergone LASIK or any other corrective eye surgery are not eligible for the post of ALP.

Nationality: A candidate must be either a citizen of India, a citizen of Nepal, a subject of Bhutan, a Tibetan refugee who came to India before 1 January 1962 with the intention of permanent settlement, or a person of Indian origin who has migrated from Pakistan, Burma, Sri Lanka, or certain East African countries as specified in the notification.

Application Fee

CategoryFeeGeneral / OBC₹500SC / ST / Female / Ex-Servicemen / Transgender / Minorities / EBC / PwBD₹250

For General and OBC candidates, ₹400 will be refunded after appearing in the 1st Stage CBT, duly deducting bank charges. For all other categories, the full fee of ₹250 will be refunded on appearing in the 1st Stage CBT. Refunds will be credited only to the bank account seeded with the candidate’s Aadhaar number, so candidates must ensure their Aadhaar is linked to an active bank account.

Fee payment is accepted only through online modes such as internet banking, debit card, credit card, or UPI. Modification of the application is allowed during 17 June 2026 to 26 June 2026 on payment of ₹250 per modification, but details filled in the “Create an Account” form (including mobile number, email ID) and the chosen RRB cannot be changed.

Selection Process

The recruitment process for Assistant Loco Pilot has four stages, and each stage must be cleared to move to the next. Marks will be normalised across shifts for all computer-based tests.

Stage 1 – First Stage CBT (CBT-1)

CBT-1 is a screening exam to shortlist candidates for CBT-2 and contains 75 multiple-choice questions for 75 marks in 60 minutes. The syllabus covers Mathematics, Mental Ability, and General Science (10th standard level). Negative marking of 1/3rd applies for each wrong answer. The minimum qualifying percentage is 40% for UR/EWS, 30% for OBC-NCL, 30% for SC, and 25% for ST. Marks scored in CBT-1 are not counted in the final merit, and shortlisting for CBT-2 is done at the rate of 15 times the vacancies notified for each RRB.

Stage 2 – Second Stage CBT (CBT-2)

CBT-2 has two parts and a total duration of 2 hours 30 minutes. Part A contains 100 questions in 90 minutes on Mathematics, General Intelligence and Reasoning, and Basic Science and Engineering. Part B contains 75 trade-specific technical questions in 60 minutes based on the candidate’s ITI trade or engineering discipline. The minimum qualifying percentage for Part A is the same as CBT-1 (40% UR/EWS, 30% OBC-NCL/SC, 25% ST), while Part B requires a minimum of 35% for all candidates irrespective of category. Negative marking of 1/3rd applies in both parts. Only Part A marks are counted for further shortlisting, provided the candidate qualifies in Part B.

Stage 3 – Computer Based Aptitude Test (CBAT)

Candidates shortlisted at eight times the number of vacancies in each community/category from CBT-2 will appear for CBAT, which tests aptitude required for the ALP role. CBAT is conducted only in English and Hindi, and there is no negative marking. A candidate must score a minimum T-score of 42 in each test battery separately to qualify. Shortlisted candidates must also submit a Self Screening Medical Declaration Report (Annexure VIII A and VIII B) signed by a General Physician and Eye Specialist from a Central or State Government Hospital or Medical College on the day of CBAT.

Stage 4 – Document Verification and Medical Examination

The final merit list is prepared by giving 50% weightage to Part A of CBT-2 and 50% weightage to CBAT scores. Candidates equal to the number of vacancies will be shortlisted for Document Verification based on their merit and zone preferences. Final appointment is subject to passing the Medical Fitness Test conducted by Railway Administration and verification of original documents and antecedents.

Medium of Examination

CBT-1 and CBT-2 questions will be available in English, Hindi, and 13 regional languages: Assamese, Bengali, Gujarati, Kannada, Konkani, Malayalam, Manipuri, Marathi, Odia, Punjabi, Tamil, Telugu, and Urdu. Questions will be displayed in the language chosen by the candidate and in English. The language choice once made at the application stage cannot be changed later. CBAT, however, is conducted only in English and Hindi.

How to Apply for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026?

Candidates must apply online through the official RRB application portal. Keep your active mobile number, valid email ID, Aadhaar number, educational and ITI/Diploma certificates, and a recent passport-size photograph ready before starting. Candidates already having an account from previous RRB CENs of 2024 or 2025 should use the same login credentials.

Step 1: Open the official application link from the Important Links section below.

Step 2: Click on “Create an Account” and register with your name, date of birth, mobile number, email ID, and Aadhaar number as recorded in your 10th class certificate. Note that no change in these details is allowed after account creation, so fill them carefully.

Step 3: Authenticate your identity using DigiLocker or Aadhaar for smoother verification at later stages. Candidates using alternate Photo IDs will face stricter scrutiny.

Step 4: Log in and fill the application form with personal, educational, and contact details. Select your preferred RRB and the order of Zone preferences carefully, since RRB choice cannot be changed once submitted. Also complete the Self-Screening Medical Declaration questionnaire.

Step 5: Capture a live photograph as prompted by the application module (no pre-uploaded photos), and upload your scanned signature in running handwriting on white paper with black ink. You can use our Image Resize Tool to adjust the signature image to the required dimensions.

Step 6: Pay the examination fee online using Internet Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI. The last date for fee payment is 16 June 2026.

Step 7: Preview all details, submit the application, and take a printout of the confirmation page for future reference.

Note: Candidates having LASIK or any other corrective eye surgery are not eligible for the post of ALP and must not apply. Self-declaration of physical and mental fitness is mandatory at the application stage itself.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

How many vacancies are available in RRB ALP Recruitment 2026?

A total of 11,127 vacancies have been notified for the post of Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP) under CEN No. 01/2026 across all participating Railway Recruitment Boards.

What is the last date to apply for RRB ALP 2026?

The last date to submit the online application is 14 June 2026 (23:59 Hrs), and the last date for fee payment is 16 June 2026. The application window opens on 15 May 2026.

What is the age limit for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026?

The age limit is 18 to 30 years as on 1 July 2026. Age relaxation is given to SC/ST (5 years), OBC-NCL (3 years), Ex-Servicemen (3 to 8 years after deducting service length), and other categories as per Railway rules.

What is the educational qualification for RRB ALP 2026?

Candidates must have passed Matriculation (10th) plus ITI in specified trades from an NCVT or SCVT recognised institution. Matriculation plus Course Completed Act Apprenticeship, or a 3-year Diploma or Degree in Mechanical, Electrical, Electronics, or Automobile Engineering is also accepted.

Can candidates with LASIK eye surgery apply for RRB ALP?

No. Candidates who have undergone LASIK or any other corrective eye surgery are not eligible for the post of Assistant Loco Pilot. A self-screening medical declaration is mandatory at the application stage.

What is the salary of an Assistant Loco Pilot?

The initial pay is ₹19,900 per month under Pay Level 2 of the 7th CPC Pay Matrix, along with Dearness Allowance, House Rent Allowance, Transport Allowance, and other allowances as per Railway rules.

How is the final merit list prepared for RRB ALP 2026?

The final merit is prepared by giving 50% weightage to Part A of CBT-2 and 50% weightage to the CBAT score, after the candidate qualifies in Part B of CBT-2 and the CBAT test batteries.

CRPF Recruitment 2026

The Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) has released an official notification for the recruitment of 9,195 Constable (Technical & Tradesmen and Pioneer) posts. Both male and female candidates who are Indian citizens and ordinarily residents of their respective States/UTs can apply. Online applications are being accepted from 20 April 2026 to 19 May 2026 through the official CRPF recruitment portal.

The recruitment will be conducted on a domicile-based pattern for Constable (Technical & Tradesmen) posts, while Constable (Pioneer) posts will be filled on an All India basis. The selection process consists of Physical Efficiency Test (PET), Physical Standard Test (PST), Computer-Based Test (CBT), Trade/Skill Test, Document Verification, and Medical Examination. The CBT will be held in English and Hindi only.

Note: Women candidates are encouraged to apply. Persons with Disabilities (PwD) are not eligible for this recruitment.

CRPF Recruitment Details

Post Name: Constable (Technical & Tradesmen) and Constable (Pioneer)

Total Vacancies: 9,195 Posts

Organization: Directorate General, Central Reserve Police Force (Ministry of Home Affairs)

Pay Scale: Pay Level-3 (₹21,700 – ₹69,100)

Educational Qualification: Matriculation or 10th Pass from a recognized Board. For CT (Motor Mechanic Vehicle), a 2-year ITI certificate in MMV trade with 1 year practical experience is also required. For CT (Driver), a valid Heavy Transport Vehicle (HTV) Driving License is required.

Age Limit (as on 01-01-2026): 18-23 years for most trades; 21-27 years for CT (Driver). Upper age relaxation of 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC, and 3 years for Ex-Servicemen (after deduction of military service) is available. You can calculate your age here.

Vacancy Breakdown

A total of 9,175 vacancies (9,096 Male and 79 Female) are available for Constable (Technical & Tradesmen) across various trades and States/UTs. Here is the trade-wise summary for male posts:

TradeMale VacanciesDriver3,176Bugler1,263Cook1,426Motor Mechanic Vehicle739Safai Karamchari547Water Carrier502Washerman363Barber231Tailor195Brass Band157Mali (Gardener)141Carpenter123Cobbler118Pipe Band83Painter32Total (Male)9,096

For female posts, 79 vacancies are available across trades such as Water Carrier (27), Brass Band (18), Pipe Band (16), Bugler (11), Hair Dresser (3), Tailor (2), Painter (1), and Mali (1).

State-wise Vacancies for Assam

Assam has a total of 288 vacancies (286 Male + 2 Female). The major trades for Assam include Driver (81), Motor Mechanic Vehicle (74), Cook (36), Bugler (30), and Safai Karamchari (14). Since Assam does not issue a Domicile/Permanent Residence Certificate, candidates from Assam are not required to submit one. However, their selection will be subject to verification of residential status from the concerned District Authorities.

Constable (Pioneer) Vacancies

20 male vacancies are available for Pioneer Wing posts: 13 for Mason and 7 for Electrician. These will be filled on All India basis. Any candidate from any State/UT can apply for these posts.

Application Fee

CategoryFeeGeneral / EWS / OBC (Male)₹100 per postSC / ST (Male)NilFemale (All Categories)NilEx-ServicemenNil

Fee can be paid online through BHIM UPI, Net Banking, Visa/MasterCard/Maestro/RuPay debit or credit cards, and CSC Wallet. Fee once paid will not be refunded.

How to Apply for CRPF Recruitment 2026?

Candidates can apply online through the official CRPF recruitment portal. Make sure you have a valid email ID, mobile number, and a recent passport-size photograph before starting the application. You can resize your photo here if needed.

Step 1: Go to the official CRPF recruitment website at rect.crpf.gov.in.

Step 2: Click on the Constable (Technical & Tradesmen and Pioneer) Recruitment 2026 link.

Step 3: Complete Step-I Registration using your email ID, mobile number, and name. You will receive your login ID and password on your registered email.

Step 4: Log out and re-login to complete Step-II. Fill in personal details, address, qualification, achievement details, and the applied post.

Step 5: Upload your scanned colour passport-size photograph (50-100 KB, JPG/JPEG, 35mm x 45mm) with the date printed on it, and signature (50-100 KB, JPG/JPEG).

Step 6: Submit the application form and pay the examination fee (₹100 for General/EWS/OBC male candidates) through Net Banking, UPI, Debit/Credit Card, or CSC Wallet.

Step 7: Take a printout of the submitted application form for your records.

Note: A candidate can apply for one post each in the Technical & Tradesmen category and Pioneer category. If applying for two posts, mention your preference. Applications with blurred photographs or signatures will be rejected. Be very careful while filling in the domicile State — no changes will be allowed after submission.

BSNL Recruitment 2026

Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL) has released the official notification for the direct recruitment of 100 Junior Telecom Officer (Telecom) posts from the open market. Eligible engineering graduates can submit their online applications through the official BSNL examination portal from 4 June 2026 to 3 July 2026. The selection will be made through a Computer-Based Test (CBT), tentatively scheduled for August 2026.

JTO is a Circle Cadre post, and recruitment is being done centrally to fill vacancies across 21 BSNL Circles. Candidates get appointed to a Circle based on their preference and merit in the examination. Before applying, candidates should read the eligibility conditions, fee structure, and selection process explained below.

BSNL Recruitment Details

Post Name: Junior Telecom Officer (Telecom) [JTO(T)-DR]

Total Vacancies: 100 Posts

Organization: Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL)

Pay Scale: IDA pay scale [E-1] of ₹16400 – ₹40500 (2nd PRC pay scale w.e.f. 01-01-2007)

Qualification: Bachelor of Engineering Degree or equivalent in Telecommunications, Electronics, Radio, Computer, Electrical, Information Technology (IT), or Instrumentation. Candidates with M.Sc. (Electronics) or M.Sc. (Computer Science) are also eligible. Those holding higher qualifications, such as M.Tech in the prescribed disciplines, may also apply.

Age Limit: Minimum 20 years and maximum 30 years as on the closing date of receipt of application. Upper age relaxation applies to reserved categories, PwBD, and BSNL employees as per Government rules. You can calculate your age here to confirm your eligibility.

Nationality: Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply. Applicants must be capable of distinguishing principal colours and should have binocular vision.

Age Relaxation

CategoryMaximum AgeUnreserved / EWS / OBC (CL)30 YearsOBC (NCL)33 YearsSC / ST35 YearsPwBD (UR/EWS/OBC-CL)40 YearsPwBD (OBC-NCL)43 YearsPwBD (SC/ST)45 Years

Ex-Servicemen and BSNL employees are entitled to age relaxation as per Central Government rules and the conditions laid down in the official notification.

Circle-wise Vacancy Breakdown

Sl. NoCircleTotal Posts1A&N12AP13Assam24Kolkata TD25Chennai TD56Chhattisgarh17Gujarat38Haryana19J&K110Karnataka2611Kerala1512MP1013MH1614NE-II315Odisha616Rajasthan117Sikkim118Tamilnadu119Telangana220Uttarakhand121UPW1Total100

Candidates can provide choices for all 21 Circles in order of preference during registration. Opting for more Circles improves the chance of selection based on marks secured. If a candidate selects fewer Circles, the candidature will be considered only for those Circles. Options once exercised are final and cannot be changed later.

Application Fee

CategoryFeeUR / OBC / EWS₹2000SC / ST / PwBD₹1000

The fee is payable only through online mode, such as Internet Banking, Credit Card, or Debit Card on the BSNL examination website. Once paid, the fee will not be refunded under any circumstances and cannot be held in reserve for any other examination.

Selection Process

Candidates have to undergo a Computer-Based Multiple Choice Objective Type Test of 3 hours duration. The test has three sectional papers: Engineering Stream Section-I, Engineering Stream Section-II, and General Ability Test Section-III. The exam carries 480 marks across 120 questions, with each correct answer awarded 4 marks and a deduction of 1 mark for each wrong answer.

SectionParticularsQuestionsMarksIEngineering Stream50200IIEngineering Stream50200IIIGeneral Ability Test2080Total120480

The minimum qualifying marks are 40% in each section and 50% overall for UR and OBC (CL) candidates. SC/ST, OBC (NCL), PwBD, and EWS candidates get a relaxation, with 35% in each section and 45% overall combined. The final merit list is prepared on the basis of total marks obtained in all three sections, provided the candidate secures the minimum qualifying marks.

Bond, Training and Probation

Selected candidates will execute a bond of ₹6 Lakhs to serve BSNL for a period of at least five years from the date of appointment. The probation period is two years. Training includes JTO Phase-I (10 weeks), Field Training (4 weeks), and JTO Phase-II specialisation course (6 weeks).

How to Apply for BSNL Recruitment 2026?

Eligible candidates must apply online through the BSNL examination portal. Keep your scanned photograph, signature, certificates, and a valid mobile number and email ID ready before starting. Follow these steps to complete your registration:

Open the official online registration link from the Important Links section below.Register using your mobile number and email ID, where an OTP will be sent to complete the process.Fill in the application form with your personal, educational, and category details.Select your Circle preferences in order of choice from the drop-down menu (up to 21 Circles).Upload your passport-size colour photograph (20kb–50kb) and signature (10kb–20kb) in jpg/jpeg format. Use our Image Resizer tool to adjust the file size.Upload the Class 10th certificate, degree certificate, mark sheets, photo ID, and category certificate as applicable.Pay the examination fee online through Internet Banking, Credit Card, or Debit Card.Submit the form and take a print-out of the computer-generated registration form for your record.

Note: An application editing window will be open from 4 July 2026 to 11 July 2026, during which candidates can edit only selective fields. In case of multiple applications, the latest registration number will be considered final.

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026

The State Bank of India (SBI), through its Central Recruitment & Promotion Department, has invited online applications from eligible Indian citizens for engagement as Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. A total of 7,150 training seats are on offer across 34 States and Union Territories under advertisement number CRPD/APPR/2026-27/07. Online registration is open from 19 May 2026 to 15 June 2026, and the online examination is tentatively scheduled for July 2026.

For Assam, 368 training seats have been allocated, with engagement options across districts, including Kamrup Metro, Dibrugarh, Cachar, Nagaon, Kamrup Rural, and others. Candidates can apply for only one state and can appear in this examination only once under this engagement project. Applicants should be proficient in any one of the specified local languages of the state they are applying for.

SBI Apprentice Recruitment Details

Post Name: Apprentice

Organization: State Bank of India (SBI)

Advertisement Number: CRPD/APPR/2026-27/07

Total Vacancies: 7,150 training seats (provisional, may vary as per actual requirement)

Vacancies in Assam: 368 seats

Qualification: Graduation from a recognised University or Institute as on 1 April 2026

Age Limit: Minimum 20 years and maximum 28 years as on 1 April 2026. The candidate must have been born not earlier than 2 April 1998 and not later than 1 April 2006. Age relaxation for reserved categories is as per the Government of India norms. You can check your age using the Age Calculator.

Training Duration: One year

Stipend: ₹15,000 per month during the engagement period. No other allowances or benefits are payable.

Apply Mode: Online

Local Language for Assam: Assamese, Bengali, or Bodo (one to be opted at the time of online application)

Note: This is an apprenticeship engagement, not employment. SBI has no obligation to offer regular employment to apprentices during or after the training period.

Vacancy Breakdown for Assam

CategoryNumber of SeatsSC25ST44OBC99EWS36UR164PwBD (VI)4PwBD (HI)4PwBD (LD)4PwBD (d&e)3Total368

PwBD reservation is horizontal within the overall vacancies. The candidate must hold a disability certificate issued by a competent authority showing not less than 40% benchmark disability.

District-wise Training Seats in Assam

Candidates from Assam can choose up to three preferred districts at the time of registration. Allotment of the final training district will be done by the Bank as per administrative requirements.

DistrictSeatsDistrictSeatsCachar28Kamrup Metro68Dima Hasao8Nagaon24Nalbari8Morigaon6Barpeta6West Karbi Anglong2Bajali4East Karbi Anglong11Baksa3Hojai11Bongaigaon10Kamrup Rural21Kokrajhar11Tamulpur3Chirang7Goalpara9Dhubri17Darrang7South Salmara-Mankachar1Hailakandi8Karimganj12Charaideo3Golaghat5Jorhat6Majuli3Sibasagar6Dibrugarh36Dhemaji6Sonitpur5Udalguri3North Lakhimpur6Biswanath Chariali4

Application Fee

CategoryFeeGeneral / OBC / EWS₹300SC / ST / PwBDNil

Fees are non-refundable and can be paid online using debit card, credit card or internet banking. Transaction charges, if any, will be borne by the candidate.

Selection Process

Selection will be based on an online written test followed by a test of the local language. Final engagement is subject to verification of eligibility documents and a medical fitness check by the Bank.

The online written test is objective in nature and carries 100 marks across four sections, each timed separately for 15 minutes. There is a negative marking of one-fourth of the marks assigned for every wrong answer.

SectionQuestionsMarksDurationGeneral / Financial Awareness252515 minutesGeneral English252515 minutesQuantitative Aptitude252515 minutesReasoning Ability & Computer Aptitude252515 minutesTotal10010060 minutes

The local language test is conducted for shortlisted candidates after the online written examination. Candidates who can produce a Class 10 or Class 12 mark sheet or certificate showing they have studied the opted local language are exempt from this test.

Examination Centres in Assam

Candidates opting for Assam can choose from the following examination centres: Dibrugarh, Guwahati, Jorhat, Silchar, and Tezpur. No request for a change of centre will be entertained once submitted.

How to Apply for SBI Apprentice Recruitment?

Before applying, candidates must register on the NAPS portal at apprenticeshipindia.gov.in. NAPS registration is mandatory and has to be completed first. After that, candidates can submit the SBI online application by following the steps below.

Open the SBI Apprentice online application link from the Important Links section below.Click on “New Registration” and enter basic details such as name, contact number and email ID to generate the registration number and password.Log in using the registration number and password, then fill in personal details, educational qualification, NAPS enrolment ID, preferred state, three preferred districts and local language choice.Upload a scanned photograph, signature, left thumb impression and a handwritten declaration in the prescribed format. Use the Image Resizer if you need to adjust the file size.Preview the application, verify all entries and pay the fee online using debit card, credit card or net banking.Submit the application and download the system-generated confirmation page for your record.

Note: Hard copy of the application is not to be sent to SBI. Candidates can apply for engagement in only one state, and multiple applications will lead to only the last valid one being retained while the fees paid for the earlier ones stand forfeited.

What is the total number of seats under the SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

SBI has notified 7,150 apprentice training seats across 34 States and Union Territories. The number is provisional and may vary based on the Bank’s actual requirement.

How many SBI Apprentice seats are available in Assam?

Assam has 368 training seats distributed across 34 districts, with the highest allocations at Kamrup Metro (68), Dibrugarh (36), Cachar (28), Nagaon (24), and Kamrup Rural (21).

What is the stipend paid to SBI Apprentices?

Selected apprentices are paid a stipend of ₹15,000 per month for the one-year engagement period. No other allowances or benefits are payable.

What is the last date to apply for the SBI Apprentice 2026?

The online application window closes on 15 June 2026. Fee payment must also be completed by the same date.

Is SBI Apprentice an SBI job?

No. This is an engagement under the Apprentices Act, 1961, and not employment with SBI. The Bank has no obligation to offer regular employment after the training period.

Is NAPS registration compulsory for SBI Apprentice?

Yes. Registration on the NAPS portal at apprenticeshipindia.gov.in is mandatory before filling the SBI online application. Candidates should also read the guidelines available on bfsissc.com.

UPSC NDA Exam 2026

The Union Public Service Commission has released the official notification for the National Defence Academy and Naval Academy Examination (II), 2026. Unmarried male and female candidates can submit their online applications through the UPSC portal until 11 June 2026 (6:00 PM). The written examination is scheduled for 13 September 2026 and will fill 394 vacancies across the Army, Navy, and Air Force wings of NDA and the 10+2 Cadet Entry Scheme of the Indian Naval Academy.

Candidates selected through this examination will join the 158th NDA Course and the 120th Indian Naval Academy Course, both starting on 1 July 2027. Before applying, candidates should read the eligibility conditions and other details carefully, since UPSC does not allow any corrections in the application after submission.

UPSC NDA II 2026 Details

Exam Name: National Defence Academy and Naval Academy Examination (II), 2026

Organization: Union Public Service Commission (UPSC)

Course Name: 158th NDA Course and 120th Indian Naval Academy Course (INAC)

Course Commencement: 1 July 2027

Total Vacancies: 394 (370 Male and 24 Female)

Nationality: Indian citizen, subject of Nepal, or person of Indian origin from specified countries with a Certificate of Eligibility

Marital Status: Only unmarried candidates are eligible

Date of Birth Range: Born not earlier than 2 January 2008 and not later than 1 January 2011

Application Fee: ₹100 (SC/ST, female candidates and wards of JCOs/NCOs/ORs studying in Military or Sainik Schools are exempt)

Exam Date: 13 September 2026

UPSC NDA II 2026 Vacancy Breakdown

Vacancies are notified separately for male and female candidates, and the written results and final merit lists will be prepared separately for both categories as per the notified vacancies.

Academy / ServiceMaleFemaleTotalNDA – Army19810208NDA – Navy (Executive)37542NDA – Air Force (Flying)90292NDA – Air Force (Ground Duties Tech)16218NDA – Air Force (Ground Duties Non-Tech)8210Naval Academy (10+2 Cadet Entry)21324Total37024394

The above vacancies are provisional and may change depending on the training capacity of the National Defence Academy and the Indian Naval Academy.

Educational Qualification

For the Army Wing of NDA, candidates must have passed Class 12 of the 10+2 pattern from a recognised State Board or University.

For the Air Force and Naval Wings of NDA and the 10+2 Cadet Entry Scheme of the Indian Naval Academy, candidates must have passed Class 12 with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics.

Candidates appearing in the 12th class examination are also eligible to apply. They must submit proof of passing by 10 June 2027. Those still in 11th class cannot apply.

Application Fee

The application fee is ₹100 and can be paid through net banking, Visa/Master/RuPay Credit or Debit Card, or UPI. The following categories are exempt from paying the fee:

CategoryFeeGeneral / OBC / EWS (Male)₹100SC / STNilFemale CandidatesNilWards of JCOs/NCOs/ORs (Military/Sainik School)Nil

Examination Pattern

The written examination has two papers, both with objective-type questions, followed by an SSB Interview for shortlisted candidates.

PaperSubjectDurationMarksPaper IMathematics2½ Hours300Paper IIGeneral Ability Test2½ Hours600SSBInterview / Personality Test–900Total1800

There is a negative marking of one-third (0.33) of the marks assigned to each question for every wrong answer in the objective papers. Question papers for Mathematics and Part B of the General Ability Test are set in both Hindi and English.

Selection Process

Selection is done in two stages. Candidates who clear the written examination are called for SSB Interview, which follows a two-stage process. Stage I includes the Officer Intelligence Rating Test and Picture Perception and Description Test. Only those who qualify Stage I move on to Stage II, which has Psychology Tests, Group Testing Officer Tasks, Interview and the Conference, spread over four days.

Candidates opting for the Air Force flying branch also have to clear the Computerised Pilot Selection System (CPSS) test. The final merit list is prepared on the basis of total marks in the written examination and the SSB Tests, subject to medical fitness.

How to Apply for UPSC NDA II 2026?

Eligible candidates can apply online following the steps below. The application has four modules – Account creation, Universal Registration (URN), Common Application Form (CAF), and the Examination Specific Module. The URN is generated once and is used for all UPSC examinations in the future.

Open the UPSC Online Application portal from the Important Links section below.Create an account and complete the Universal Registration (URN) profile with your personal details and Aadhaar information.Fill the Common Application Form, upload your photograph, signature and capture a live photograph as instructed. You can resize photos using the Image Resizer tool.Open the Examination Specific Module for NDA II 2026 and fill in your service preferences (1 to 4) and exam centre.Pay the application fee of ₹100 through net banking, Credit/Debit Card or UPI. SC/ST candidates, female candidates and eligible wards of JCOs/NCOs/ORs are exempt.Submit the form and take a printout of the application for your records.

Note: Once submitted, no correction, alteration or withdrawal of the application is allowed. Candidates should also note that the entry into the examination venue closes 30 minutes before the start of each session, and face authentication is mandatory at the venue.

After the written exam, candidates can check the e-Admit Card details on the UPSC NDA Admit Card page, and once results are out, qualified candidates can track them through the UPSC NDA Result page.

Important Dates of UPSC NDA II 2026

CTET Notification 2026

The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has released the official CTET Notification for the 22nd edition of the Central Teacher Eligibility Test. The exam is scheduled for 6 September 2026 (Sunday) and will be conducted in 27 languages across 132 cities in India. Eligible candidates can submit their online applications from 11 May 2026 to 10 June 2026 through the official CTET website.

CTET is conducted for persons who want to become teachers for Classes I to VIII in schools. Paper I is for those aspiring to teach Classes I to V, while Paper II is for those who want to teach Classes VI to VIII. Candidates planning to teach both levels need to appear in both papers.

CTET Exam Details

Exam Name: Central Teacher Eligibility Test (CTET)

Conducting Body: Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE)

Papers: Paper I (Classes I to V) and Paper II (Classes VI to VIII)

Exam Date: 6 September 2026 (Sunday)

Exam Mode: Offline (OMR based)

Duration: 2 hours 30 minutes per paper

Total Questions: 150 MCQs (1 mark each, no negative marking)

Question Paper Language: Bilingual (Hindi/English)

Qualifying Marks: 60% or above

Validity: Lifetime for all categories

CTET 2026 Exam Schedule

PaperShiftTimingPaper IIMorning09:30 AM to 12:00 NoonPaper IEvening02:30 PM to 05:00 PM

If the number of candidates increases, the exam may also be conducted on 5 September 2026 (Saturday).

CTET 2026 Eligibility Criteria

The minimum qualifications for appearing in CTET are notified by the National Council for Teacher Education (NCTE). Candidates should check the NCTE website to confirm their eligibility before applying. A relaxation of up to 5% in the qualifying marks of the minimum educational qualification is allowed to candidates belonging to SC, ST, OBC, and Differently-abled categories.

Eligibility for Paper I (Classes I to V)

Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in the final year of 2-year Diploma in Elementary Education, OR 4-year Bachelor of Elementary Education (B.El.Ed), OR 2-year Diploma in Education (Special Education), OR a Bachelor’s Degree with at least 50% marks along with B.Ed qualification as per NCTE norms.

Eligibility for Paper II (Classes VI to VIII)

Bachelor’s Degree and passed or appearing in the final year of 2-year Diploma in Elementary Education, OR Bachelor’s Degree with at least 50% marks and B.Ed, OR Senior Secondary with at least 50% marks and 4-year B.El.Ed/B.A.Ed/B.Sc.Ed, OR equivalent qualifications as per NCTE norms.

CTET 2026 Exam Pattern

Both Paper I and Paper II contain 150 multiple-choice questions of one mark each. There is no negative marking. The duration of each paper is 2 hours 30 minutes.

Paper I Structure (Classes I to V)

SubjectMCQsMarksChild Development and Pedagogy3030Mathematics3030Environmental Studies3030Language I3030Language II3030Total150150

Paper II Structure (Classes VI to VIII)

SubjectMCQsMarksChild Development and Pedagogy3030Mathematics & Science OR Social Studies/Social Science6060Language I3030Language II3030Total150150

CTET 2026 Application Fee

The fee can be paid through Debit Card, Credit Card, or Net Banking. GST will be charged extra by the bank. Fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.

CategoryOnly Paper I or IIBoth Paper I & IIGeneral / OBC (NCL)₹1000₹1200SC / ST / Differently Abled Person₹500₹600

Languages Offered for CTET 2026

Candidates have to select two languages from the list of 27 available languages. Language II must be different from Language I. The languages available are English, Hindi, Assamese, Bengali, Garo, Gujarati, Kannada, Khasi, Malayalam, Manipuri, Marathi, Mizo, Nepali, Odia, Punjabi, Sanskrit, Tamil, Telugu, Tibetan, Urdu, Bodo, Dogri, Kashmiri, Konkani, Maithili, Santhali, and Sindhi.

How to Apply for CTET 2026?

Before starting the application, keep a scanned photograph (10 KB to 100 KB, 3.5 cm × 4.5 cm) and signature (3 KB to 30 KB, 3.5 cm × 1.5 cm) ready in JPG/JPEG format. Use the Application Form link given in the Important Links section below to start the application.

Step 1: Click on the Application Form link given in the Important Links section below.Step 2: Fill the authentication form with State, Identity type, Name, Date of Birth, and Gender as per Class X certificate.Step 3: Complete the online application form and create a password (8 to 13 characters with one uppercase, one lowercase, one number, and one special character).Step 4: Note down the Registration Number / Application Number generated by the system.Step 5: Upload the scanned photograph and signature in JPG/JPEG format.Step 6: Pay the examination fee through Debit Card, Credit Card, or Net Banking.Step 7: Take a printout of the Confirmation Page for your record.

Note: If a candidate submits more than one online application, his/her candidature will be cancelled and the candidate may also be debarred from future examinations. Online correction in particulars will be allowed only from 15 June 2026 to 18 June 2026.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

When will the CTET 2026 exam be conducted?

The CTET 2026 exam will be conducted on 6 September 2026 (Sunday). If the number of candidates increases, the exam may also be held on 5 September 2026 (Saturday).

What is the last date to apply for CTET 2026?

The last date to submit the online application and pay the examination fee is 10 June 2026 up to 11:59 PM.

Can I choose my exam city while applying for CTET 2026?

No, there is no provision to select the examination city on the online application portal. CBSE will allot the city on a random basis, and no change request will be accepted.

What is the qualifying mark for CTET 2026?

A candidate who scores 60% or more in the CTET exam will be considered as TET pass. School managements may give concessions to SC, ST, OBC, and Differently abled candidates as per their reservation policy.

What is the validity of the CTET certificate?

The CTET qualifying certificate is valid for a lifetime for all categories. There is no restriction on the number of attempts a candidate can take.

Is there negative marking in CTET 2026?

No, there is no negative marking in CTET. Each question carries one mark, and candidates can attempt all 150 questions without any penalty for wrong answers.
